summary: Backend API regression passed on public endpoint; frontend mobile web is intermittently unstable due Expo tunnel/Metro bundle disconnects, blocking reliable end-to-end UI validation.

ui_bugs.component: Auth/Home transition on Expo web preview
ui_bugs.issue: Intermittent blank/splash/login-only rendering due failed bundle fetch (502) and Metro disconnect during interaction.
Missing testID attributes on buttons/inputs/tabs makes RN web automation brittle and hard to stabilize.

integration_issues.issue: Flow works intermittently, but repeated runs disconnect from Metro and revert to auth/blank state mid-test.

react_native_issues.issue: Console repeatedly warns that non-route files inside app/ are treated as routes; constants/images route warning persists.

action_items: Stabilize Expo tunnel/Metro web preview availability (bundle endpoint currently returns intermittent 502 / disconnect).
Move non-route helper files out of app/ routes scope or make them private route-safe files to eliminate expo-router route conflicts.
Add testID to all interactive RN elements (auth inputs/buttons, language toggle, tabs, action buttons, settings controls).
After frontend stabilization, re-run full UI regression for refresh/favorites/history/settings/push flows.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses fallback JWT secret (JWT_SECRET default string) which is unsafe if env is missing.
Frontend API base URL source differs from required EXPO_BACKEND_URL convention; currently depends on EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL/extra.backendUrl.
Expo-router warnings indicate architecture smell: many non-route files are located under app/ and discovered as routes.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 TEST_ signup user via /api/auth/signup during pytest run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use public URL only. Login with text selector worked once and reached home, but repeated runs dropped Metro connection. Backend endpoints are healthy and passed full pytest suite.
rca_of_issue: Primary blocker is preview infrastructure instability: bundle URL for expo-router intermittently returns 502 with MIME mismatch and Metro disconnect (1006), causing blank or auth-only UI and preventing deterministic selector execution. Secondary issue is expo-router route pollution from non-route files in app/.
